UK’s bilateral aid to African countries, which funds areas such as schools and clinics, to be cut by almost £900m by 2028-29

Some of the world’s poorest countries will lose out on UK aid that funds programmes such as schools and clinics, due to budget cuts set out by the foreign secretary.

The UK’s bilateral aid to African countries will be reduced by almost £900m by 2028-29 – a 56% cut – as part of more than £6bn in cuts which are funding an increase in defence spending.

events.summary:
Lemi Limbu, who has severe intellectual disabilities, remains in prison and will now face retrial for the murder of her daughter

A woman with severe intellectual disabilities in Tanzania has had her conviction and death sentence quashed after spending more than a decade in prison awaiting execution.

Lemi Limbu, now in her early 30s, was convicted of the murder of her daughter in 2015. On 4 March, a court in Shinyanga, northern Tanzania, declared she can appeal. She will face a retrial, but a date has yet to be set.

Nigeria had largest increase in terrorism-related deaths, ranking fourth in global index behind Pakistan, Burkina Faso and Niger

Jihadist violence rose sharply in Nigeria and Democratic Republic of Congo last year, even as global deaths from terrorism dropped to their lowest level in a decade, according to a new report.

Nigeria recorded the largest increase in terrorism deaths globally in 2025, with fatalities rising by 46% from 513 in 2024 to 750, placing it fourth in the Global Terrorism Index, behind Pakistan, Burkina Faso and Niger.

A former Belgian diplomat, 93, should stand trial over alleged complicity in the 1961 murder of Patrice Lumumba, the first prime minister of what was then the newly independent Congolese state, a Brussels court has ruled.

Étienne Davignon, the only person still alive among 10 Belgians the Lumumba family accuses of involvement in the killing, is charged with participation in war crimes.

The illegal transfer of Lumumba and his associates from Léopoldville (now Kinshasa) to Katanga.

The “humiliating and degrading treatment” of the men.

Depriving them of a fair trial.

At least 23 people have been killed and more than 100 others injured in multiple suspected suicide bombings in the north-eastern Nigerian city of Maiduguri, shattering its reputation as a relative oasis of calm in recent years as a long-running insurgency was pushed to the rural hinterlands.

Authorities said the explosions went off at the post office and market areas, as well as the entrance to the University of Maiduguri teaching hospital, on Monday evening during iftar, the breaking of fast in the month of Ramadan.

South Korean president Lee Jae Myung warned at a cabinet meeting this week that “the issue is safety” and urged heightened vigilance by the interior ministry and emergency services to prepare for every possibility. He described the concert as an important occasion to reaffirm the country’s global cultural standing.

China has not yet commented on the delay to the highly anticipated trip, in which Trump and the Chinese president, Xi Jinping, will meet in person for the first time since October. Trump previously said he hoped to delay the trip, originally scheduled to run from 31 March to 2 April, for “five or six weeks”.

Across south-east Asia, governments are scrambling to find ways to conserve energy and shield the public from soaring costs as war in the Middle East causes what the International Energy Agency has described as the largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market.

Iran said on Thursday it would show “zero restraint” if its energy infrastructure was targeted again as Qatar revealed that almost a fifth of its liquefied natural gas export capacity had been knocked out in an Iranian strike that is likely to have a years-long impact.

The warning, delivered by the Iranian foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, followed Israel’s attack on Iran’s massive South Pars gasfield – which it shares with Qatar – which triggered Iranian retaliatory strikes on Qatar’s Ras Laffan gas complex and other Gulf neighbours, sending stock markets tumbling globally and triggering sharp increases in gas prices.

Writing in the Economist, Badr Albusaidi, the Omani minister who mediated the latest nuclear talks between Iran and the US, offered an unusually damning assessment of events leading up to the US and Israel’s bombing of Iran and the war it has triggered across the Middle East.

events.title: West Point analysis warns that strait of Hormuz blockade will strangle US defense industry

The closure of the strait of Hormuz is causing a “paralyzing, real-time problem” for any prospective manufacturing surge in the US defense industrial base, and even for the repair of defense equipment damaged by Iranian attacks, according to analysis published by West Point’s Modern War Institute.

In particular sulphur, a vital upstream input in the extraction of critical minerals including copper and cobalt, has seen a “near total” disruption of seaborne trade in the straits, which makes up half the world’s total shipments, and prices have spiked nearly 25% since the war began, and seen a 165% rise year on year, the report said.

events.title: ‘Waiting for days’: India feels impact of gas supply chain disruption amid Iran conflict
events.summary:
People struggle to cook and businesses bear brunt as closure of strait of Hormuz slows imports of liquefied petroleum gas

For four days, Maya Rani, 36, has been arriving each morning at a gas distributor’s office in Delhi, her six-month-old daughter in her lap, waiting for hours. And each day she returns home empty-handed, told that a cooking gas cylinder may not be available for at least another week. Around her, the queue keeps growing, people clutching forms and documents, hoping to secure a cylinder.

The flame in her kitchen began to fade last week and her husband, as he always does, took their 5kg cylinder to a local refiller. This time, there was nothing. The only option left was to apply for a government-subsidised supply, a process that has meant repeated visits, long waits and no certainty.

Pakistan has announced a five-day pause in strikes against neighbouring Afghanistan, as a mass funeral was held for some of the hundreds of victims killed in Monday’s attack on a drug rehabilitation centre in Kabul.

The Afghan Taliban government has said more than 400 people were killed and 265 others wounded in that attack, which took place as people at the centre were praying days before the end of the holy month of Ramadan.

The strategy is to impose such a steep cost on the Taliban administration that they act to prevent attacks emanating from Afghanistan. Yet it carries the risk of spiralling violence.

Afghan rescue crews were still digging bodies out of the rubble on Tuesday after the strike, the deadliest single attack so far in a three-week war between the two countries.

The Ministry of Justice has admitted it has known for almost two decades that about a quarter of prison places are unsafe, putting the people housed in affected cells at risk.

Last June, the House of Commons voted to end the criminalisation of women who terminate their pregnancies outside of the legal framework, while keeping the existing framework in place. Doctors and others who act outside of the law could still face the threat of prosecution.

Speaking in the Oval Office on Thursday, Trump said he had spoken to Israel’s Benjamin Netanyahu following the strikes on Iran’s South Pars gasfield – part of a reserve shared with Qatar – and had told the Israeli prime minister to refrain from further attacks that could escalate a regional war on energy infrastructure.

events.summary: How high will the Iran war drive up the prices of oil, gasoline and diesel? There is great concern that the price shock could dash hopes for economic recovery in Germany.

events.title: Honda warns of $16bn hit on its pivot away from EVs
events.summary: Japan's Honda expects to book extra costs of almost $16 billion as it recalibrates its electric vehicle strategy. It blamed US policy changes and tariffs in part, as well as reduced competitiveness in Asia.

events.title: Elon Musk's Tesla experiment: Unchecked ambition costs lives
events.summary: A documentary explores how Tesla drivers serve as unpaid beta testers of the company's self-driving cars while Elon Musk focuses on his long-term goal: colonizing Mars.

events.title: WAFCON 2026: A 'combination of factors' behind postponement
events.summary: African football's governing body CAF says it made the decision to postpone it's top women's tournament to ensure "the best possible conditions." It's not the first time the tournament has been moved at short notice.

events.title: Germany news: Lufthansa pilots stage another strike
events.summary: A two-day strike by Lufthansa pilots is expected to lead to many cancellations. The German chancellor is visiting Norway, a key energy supplier to his country.

events.title: How Poland is flexing its economic muscle in Western Europe
events.summary: Poland's economy is now a European star performer. A new trend suggests further momentum, with more and more Polish firms acquiring foreign companies, including in Germany.

events.title: Trump's arch plan and the history of gateways
events.summary: The proposed 250‑foot arch has sparked debate in Washington. But monumental gateways have shaped politics, memory, and ritual across cultures for millennia.

events.title: Merz says Germany won't join EU return to nuclear energy
events.summary: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen is in favor of a proposal to build new nuclear power plants in the EU. German Chancellor Friedrich Merz says it's impossible.

events.title: Germany, others partially release oil reserves amid Iran war
events.summary: Germany, Austria, Japan, Spain and France have all indicated plans to release some of their oil reserves, a day after the International Energy Agency was debating the pros and cons. The Iran war has driven prices up.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Jordan fans frustrated by US visa hurdles
events.summary: Jordan is set to make its World Cup debut, but military conflict and the US government may keep fans at home. Those wishing to travel with their team are calling for answers on visas from FIFA and the US.

events.title: Iran war roils oil trade, casting doubt on US fossil fuel push
events.summary: The Iran war has sent oil and gas prices surging. As countries like the US double down on fossil fuels, experts say the conflict shows how speeding up homegrown renewables is the only way to avoid future energy crises.

events.title: Germany news: Pilots at Lufthansa to stage two-day strike
events.summary: Lufthansa pilots have called a two-day strike amid a dispute over pension payments. Meanwhile, Volkswagen says it plans to cut 50,000 jobs by 2030.

events.title: BioNTech founders step down to start new venture
events.summary: Ugur Sahin and Özlem Türeci were instrumental in developing the first COVID-19 vaccine to be approved in the West. Now they say they "are ready to become pioneers once again."

events.title: Discrimination is a widespread phenomenon in Germany
events.summary: At the supermarket, when looking for a flat, at work: millions of people in Germany are victims of discrimination. A new study reveals just how bad the problem is.

events.title: Volkswagen Group profits take big hit on Porsche shift
events.summary: Strategic readjustment at Porsche, as well as dwindling sales in both main export markets, the US and China took a big bite out of VW's 2025 profits — which almost halved.

events.title: Iran war: How long before Gulf nations stop pumping oil?
events.summary: The oil price spiked near $120 after strikes on Iran's energy sites and the shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz. With tankers stuck and Gulf oil facilities hit, producers are running short on storage to maintain production.

events.title: AI lab Anthropic sues to block Pentagon blacklisting
events.summary: Tech start-up Anthropic has refused to meet the Pentagon's demands for unrestricted military use of AI, setting up a legal showdown with the Trump administration.

events.title: Germany: Chancellor Merz downplays a state election defeat
events.summary: The Green party scored a surprise win at the state election in Baden-Württemberg. The results came as a blow to the conservative CDU and the center-left SPD, who are still trying to make sense of what happened.

events.title: Germany: AfD marks success in state election, despite scandals
events.summary: In the state election in Baden-Württemberg, the far-right AfD almost doubled its result to just under 19%. However, given the numerous crises within the party, the celebrations remain subdued.

events.title: Fears grow for Iran's women's football team
events.summary: Calls are growing for Australia to offer asylum to the Iranian national team at the Asian Cup. With serious punishment looming, five players have reportedly escaped Iran's supervision amid safety concerns.

events.title: Long before AI, fake photos were already popular
events.summary: From anti-Nazi photo montages to staged photos, an exhibition in Amsterdam focuses on the long history of manipulated visuals.

events.title: US allows India's Russian oil purchases again
events.summary: Less than a month after India agreed to stop buying Russian oil in exchange for a US trade deal, Washington says it can buy it again. The latest reports suggest it may loosen other sanctions against Moscow.

events.title: Plasticizer chemical levels in German children raise concern
events.summary: Tests in Germany show children and teens are contaminated with banned plasticizers. You may never have heard of these potentially toxic chemicals, but they're everywhere, including clothes, cosmetics and food packaging.

events.title: One in three Gen Z men want obedient women
events.summary: Many young men embrace deeply conservative gender roles, a new international study finds. Is social media stoking the return of traditionalism?

events.title: International Women's Day: Workplace equality needs action
events.summary: Women in Germany who wish to have careers and families face systemic challenges. The German sociologist Jutta Allmendinger sees reason for optimism, with countries like Iceland showing the way.

events.title: Why all the hype for 'The Devil Wears Prada 2'?
events.summary: Fans are eagerly awaiting the sequel ― and the return of those iconic characters and their vicious power plays. But "The Devil Wears Prada 2" also shows us just how much fashion and media have changed since 2006.

events.title: Formula 1 in 2026: Just Formula E on steroids?
events.summary: Formula 1 is kicking off the new season with some big rule changes. Drivers will have to focus on energy management—and not everyone is thrilled about it. Plus, there are two new teams: Audi and Cadillac.

events.title: Ecosystem collapse could fuel the next global security crisis
events.summary: Debt-for-nature swaps and conservation funds to halt biodiversity loss are gaining traction as governments link ecosystem collapse to geopolitical instability.

events.title: Michelangelo: The man, the brand, the mystery
events.summary: The Renaissance artist's name recently made headlines as claims of a secret deathbed trove of artworks and a rediscovered bust surfaced. But what do we actually know about his life?

events.title: F1's Laura Müller continuing to blaze a trail for women
events.summary: Laura Müller is Formula 1's first female race engineer, taking crucial decisions for Haas driver Esteban Ocon. On International Women's Day, Turn 6 at Melbourne's Albert Park race track is to be named after her.

events.title: India news: India reach T20 final in shootout with England
events.summary: India's 253 runs in Mumbai proved just enough to best England and reach the cricket T20 World Cup final. Meanwhile, Finland's Alexander Stubb is in Delhi and polls have closed in neighboring Nepal.

events.title: Football: Women's AFCON postponed at 11th hour
events.summary: After weeks of uncertainty, football's governing body in Africa has announced the postponement of the Women's Africa Cup of Nations, which had been slated to start in mid-March.

events.title: Paralympic Winter Games: What you need to know
events.summary: Just weeks after hosting the Winter Olympics, northern Italy is set to host the Winter Paralympic Games. DW fills you in on what you need to know.

events.title: Salute replaces silence for Iran after 'message from home'
events.summary: In a change from the silence of their first game, Iran's women's national team saluted their anthem against Australia on Thursday. It appears to be another case of authorities cracking down on sport as a tool of protest.

events.title: Tricia Tuttle to remain Berlinale head — with new code of conduct
events.summary: Facing political backlash following Pro-Palestinian speeches during the festival's closing gala, Tuttle received widespread support from the international film industry.

events.title: Iran strikes highlight Dubai influencers' free speech limits
events.summary: Dubai influencers are accused of generating "tone-deaf" social media posts as they react — or avoid reacting — to the Iranian missile attacks on the United Arab Emirates.

events.title: Iran war: UN's red line on force tested again
events.summary: The recent US-Israel attack on Iran spotlights the UN Charter, drafted after World War II. Article 2(4) stands out in the current situation: It bans unprovoked threats and the use of force amongst countries.

events.title: Iran attacks on Gulf oil and gas sites trigger energy fears
events.summary: Iranian attacks on regional refineries and gas facilities, and oil tankers are increasing global energy supply fears, risking major economic fallouts from Asia to Europe, and leaving consumers bracing for higher prices.

events.title: Why it's worth getting a heat pump if you live in a cold country, too
events.summary: Worldwide, people are increasingly turning to heat pumps to warm their home. The devices save money, and they're good for the environment. In Northern Europe, they've long proved their worth in colder climates.

events.title: World Cup 2026: US-Iran war adds to list of concerns
events.summary: With 100 days to go until World Cup 2026 starts, the war between co-host USA and qualifiers Iran poses serious questions. Spiraling prices, safety and reluctant host cities are among the other unresolved issues.

events.title: How sport is being disrupted by the US-Israel war with Iran
events.summary: The war against Iran launched by the United States and Israel — and Iran's retaliation — have had quite an impact on sports. DW looks at some of the conflict's current effects, and at what could be ahead.

events.title: Ukraine war: German parts make their way into Russian drones
events.summary: Hundreds of thousands of German parts have been found in drones used by Russia to attack Ukraine, despite sanctions. The Russian military apparently values German components highly.

events.title: Will Iran war send oil prices above $100 a barrel?
events.summary: Oil markets are bracing for sharp price spikes after the US-Israel attacks on Iran and Tehran's retaliation. Iran's role as a major producer matters, but its strategic position is keeping traders on edge.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz halts after US‑Israel attack on Iran
events.summary: US President Trump has carried out his threat to attack Iran, shifting attention to the Strait of Hormuz, the world's most critical oil chokepoint. Iran has repeatedly threatened to block the vital waterway.

events.title: Vincent Kompany: Bayern Munich's coach with a cause
events.summary: Vincent Kompany is preparing for a Bundesliga title fight. But the Belgian has also been speaking out after the racism suffered by Real Madrid’s Vinicius Jnr, a cause that has been formative in his life and career.

events.title: Pentagon pressures Anthropic in escalating AI showdown
events.summary: The row between Anthropic, a much vaunted AI startup, and the Trump administration, is sharply escalating. The Pentagon wants complete access to its models, despite safety and ethical concerns.

events.title: Netflix bows out of Warner Bros. bid, Paramount set to win
events.summary: Netflix has dropped out of a bid for Warner Bros, paving the way for Paramount Skydance to acquire its renowned Hollywood rival. The takeover will significantly change the Hollywood and US media landscape.

events.title: New HIV drug may end multi-pill regimen for older people
events.summary: People who have lived with HIV long-term can need up to a dozen pills every day to suppress the virus. Results from a new drug trial raise hope of changing that.

events.title: Berlinale future hangs in the balance amidst polarized Gaza debate
events.summary: After an emergency meeting, the fate of the film festival head Tricia Tuttle remains uncertain. A Palestinian award-winner's accusation that Germany supports genocide had sparked political backlash.

events.title: Europe bags savings as America chases fossil‑fuel nostalgia
events.summary: While Washington talks up fossil fuels, EU countries are cashing in on efficiency and low‑cost renewables.

events.title: Beyond oil and gas: Russia's nuclear leverage explained
events.summary: Russian oil and gas face sanctions, but nuclear energy is another story. DW shows how far Russia's influence extends, and why countries continue to turn to it for nuclear energy.

events.title: The DIY solar hack arriving in US homes
events.summary: Americans are embracing easy, plug‑and‑play solar units that slash energy costs — even as Washington tries to slow the clean‑energy shift.

events.title: Is sustainable travel possible in times of mass tourism?
events.summary: Travel can drive overtourism, contribute to climate change and support problematic political systems. Yet the industry also creates livelihoods and fosters exchange.

events.title: Football: Afghanistan women again set to fight for future
events.summary: Afghanistan's female players are used to fighting and waiting. Months after their reintroduction to internationals, they now wait on another game and must wrestle with FIFA doubling down on a country that rejected them.

events.title: When someone stole credit — and won prizes — for an animated movie made by two German students, the duo found a unique way to fight back
events.summary: "Butty" became "T-130": An American submitted a movie by two German film students to film festivals under his own name and won prizes. The duo dealt with the matter in an unusual way.

events.title: Clashes spark safety concerns for World Cup in Mexico
events.summary: A wave of violence set off by the killing of a Mexican drug cartel boss has raised concerns about security ahead of the World Cup. FIFA and Mexican offiicals have played down any risk.

events.title: Hungary, Slovakia clash with Ukraine over oil pipeline
events.summary: The Druzhba pipeline serves as the primary conduit for Russian crude oil deliveries to Hungary and Slovakia. Shipments through the pipeline have stalled since the end of January.

events.title: NASA's Artemis II moon rocket back to the hangar
events.summary: A series of fueling issues has delayed the much-anticipated launch of NASA's Artemis II moon mission, the first to carry astronauts since the Apollo moon landings. The Space Launch System rocket is grounded until April.

events.title: China cashes in on clean energy as Trump clings to coal
events.summary: The Trump administration has rolled back environmental protections and blocked green energy development, China is forging ahead.

events.title: Do not inhale! How wildfire smoke 'affects the whole body'
events.summary: Wildfires destroy lives and livelihoods. But survivors and firefighters also suffer long-term harm to their health. A growing body of research suggests particulate matter (PM2.5) in wildfire smoke is a major culprit.

events.title: Winter Olympics closing ceremony draws curtain on Games
events.summary: A distinctly Italian mix of opera, dance and DJs was mixed with a few last gold medals as Italy passed the Winter Olympic flame to France. Here's how it happened.

events.title: Berlinale: 'Yellow Letters' wins the Golden Bear award
events.summary: The political drama by Oscar nominee Ilker Catak took the top award at the Berlin International Film Festival.

events.title: Giant tortoises reintroduced to a Galapagos island
events.summary: Ecuador released 158 juveniles in the first phase of an ambitious rewilding effort at the World Heritage Site.

events.title: Mercury fallout: What coal emissions do to people
events.summary: Coal-fired power plants are a major source of mercury contamination for people and the environment. Here's what you need to know.

events.title: Winter Olympics 2026: The lasting impressions
events.summary: Milan Cortina will be remembered for the performances of a Norwegian superstar and a Ukrainian skeleton racer's "helmet of remembrance."

events.title: 'Remarkable' new cat cancer genome could benefit humans
events.summary: International team uncovers gene mutations that cause rare and aggressive cancer in both cats and humans. Their findings could provide a path to treatments for both species.

events.title: Brazil's soy giants clear way for deforestation
events.summary: For almost two decades, Brazil's largest soy producers guaranteed their products did not come from land cleared in the Amazon rainforest. Now, all bets are off.

events.title: Skimo: What you need to know about the newest Olympic sport
events.summary: Ski mountaineering is making its Olympic debut at the Winter Games in Italy. What is this sport all about?

events.title: Charli xcx spoofs herself in 'The Moment'
events.summary: She couldn't identify with the current trend of concert films, so British pop star Charli xcx flipped the form on a traditional genre with her new mockumentary.

events.title: For a healthy break, don't check your ex's social media
events.summary: It feels harmless to peek at an ex’s posts on social media. But studies show that repeated checking can intensify heartbreak, prolong longing and slow emotional recovery.

events.title: US reportedly weighing military reinforcements as Iran war enters possible new phase
events.summary: President Donald Trump's administration is considering deploying thousands of U.S. troops to reinforce its operation in the Middle East, as the U.S. military prepares for ​possible next steps in its campaign against Iran, Reuters reports quoting a U.S. official and three people familiar with the matter. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with Michael O’Hanlon, Director of Research in the Foreign Policy Programme at the Brookings Institution

events.title: Video of Iranian soldier being targeted in a Tehran strike is AI generated
events.summary: Since Tuesday, videos supposedly showing Iranian soldiers being bombed in Tehran have been widely shared by pro-Israel accounts, Israeli media and even an official Israeli government social media account. But doubts have surfaced over their authenticity, as Emerald Maxwell explains.

events.title: Senegal not losing without a fight
events.summary: In tonight's program, a joint statement from the Democratic Republic of Congo and Rwanda, where both nations have agreed to take steps to advance last year's stalled peace accord. Also, Senegal says it's not losing its 2025 Africa Cup of Nations title without a fight. And in the Kenyan capital, a groundbreaking menstrual leave policy for government employees is making a difference and proving a success.

events.title: Gas prices soar as energy infrastructure is attacked in Gulf
events.summary: Natural gas prices soared, as attacks intensified on energy infrastracture in the Middle East. The Ras Laffan plant in Qatar has sustained 'significant damage,' according to its operators, which could take years to fully repair. That will have a significant impact on global supplies, because the facility produces around a fifth of the world's LNG. Also in the show - the World Trade Organization warns a trade slowdown could lead to mounting food insecurity.

events.title: French Socialists split over alliance with hard-left party in mayoral runoffs
events.summary: Several candidates from France’s mainstream left have joined forces with Jean-Luc Mélenchon’s France Unbowed to boost their chances in mayoral runoffs on Sunday, even as others reject any association with the hard-left party, exposing a deepening divide in the French left ahead of next year's presidential election.

events.title: Orbán blocks €90bn loan, threatening vital EU support for Ukraine’s energy system & overall survival
events.summary: Mark Owen is pleased to welcome Dr. Hanna Shelest, Director of Security Studies and Global Outreach Programmes at the Foreign Policy Council 'Ukrainian Prism' and Editor-in-chief at UA: Ukraine Analytica. According to Dr. Hanna Shelest, Ukrainian forces demonstrate that with adaptive strategy, technological innovation, and timely support, it is possible not only to resist but to go on the offensive. Yet this capacity is inseparable from sustained international backing, both military and financial.

events.title: Netanyahu seeks to appease Trump with latest Iran remarks to the press
events.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday denied what he called "fake news" that Israel had dragged US President Donald Trump into war with Iran. "Does anyone really think that someone can tell President Trump what to do?" Netanyahu told journalist at a press conference. Reporting from Jerusalem, FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky explains that Netanyahu's remarks were directed to an audience of one: Donald Trump.

events.title: Netanyahu’s Iran war press briefing targeted at ‘audience of one’: Donald Trump
events.summary: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu’s press briefing Thursday on the Iran war was targeted at an “audience of one”, says FRANCE 24’s Noga Tarnopolsky, reporting from Jerusalem, noting that his message was directed at President Donald Trump. The briefing provided a “behind-the-scenes peek” into “the manner in which Prime Minister Netanyahu framed the Iran situation for Donald Trump,” Tarnopolsky notes.

events.title: Replay: Israeli PM Netanyahu addresses press conference
events.summary:
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday defended Israel’s and the US’s campaign against Iran, saying that airstrikes have largely destroyed Iran’s capacity to enrich uranium or produce ballistic missiles.
 
Speaking at a press conference, Netanyahu declared, “We are winning, and Iran is being decimated,” highlighting the ongoing destruction of missile and nuclear weapons production facilities.

events.title: Trump's grounds for concerns: Will the US risk lives to take Iran’s uranium?
events.summary: The Middle East War is on its 19th day, the next phase could well be its most decisive. With a perceived split in US and Israeli strategy now emerging. If we can believe Donald Trump’s words. He says Israel bomned Iran’s gas field “without his knowledge.Then Trump, perhaps playing catch up, threatens to “blow up” - his phrase - Iran’s South Pars gasfield if Iran strikes Qatar. A move experts say would literally explode the region, sending a backdraft into Qatar. A remark that speaks loudly of a lack of understanding from Trump and his advisors... Iran is striking the Gulf allies of the United States because the US & Israel are bombing Iran… It’s like Catch 22.

events.title: ‘It was very painful’: Yazidi woman testifies on IS group sexual slavery in Paris court
events.summary: A Yazidi woman held as a sex slave by the Islamic State (IS) group recounted her harrowing ordeal before a French court Thursday, detailing her abduction, repeated rapes and forced sales. The trial of French jihadist Sabri Essid, prosecuted in absentia for genocide and crimes against humanity, marks France’s first case on IS group atrocities against the Yazidis.

events.title: French tycoon Bolloré to face corruption trial over elections in Togo and Guinea
events.summary: French billionaire Vincent Bolloré is set to face trial on corruption and embezzlement charges linked to election campaigns in Togo and Guinea between 2009 and 2011, prosecutors said on Thursday. Bolloré, the former head of his family’s eponymous multinational group, is accused of undercharging for services provided to presidential candidates in both countries in exchange for port contracts.

events.title: Senegal stripped of AFCON title: Reactions in Dakar
events.summary: In a shock, historic decision, the Confederation of African Football (CAF) announced that it is withdrawing the Africa Cup of Nations trophy from Senegal, awarding it instead to Morocco. The decision follows complaints from the host nation Moroccan, after a highly contested final. The news has left many Senegalese in shock, torn between confusion and anger, as FRANCE 24’s team in Dakar reports.

events.title: 'Idea that Trump can be led around by Israel is foolishness,' ex-envoy to Iran says
events.summary: In an interview with FRANCE 24, Elliott Abrams, a former special envoy of US President Donald Trump to Iran, dismissed as "foolishness" the idea that Israel led Trump into war with Tehran. Abrams, who served during Trump's first term in office, said he backed the ongoing war, saying that "five presidents in a row" had vowed to prevent a nuclear Iran but Trump "is the first person who's really acting on that".

events.title: Mumbai celebrates local new year
events.summary: #Mumbai rang with drums as it celebrated the #GudiPadwa festival, which marks the start of the new year in #Marathi culture and the arrival of spring. "Gudi" references the flag of Lord Brahma, creator of the universe according to Hindus, and "Padwa" means the first day of the lunar phase.

events.title: Settler violence surges in the West Bank
events.summary: As the Iran-Israel-US-Lebanon war makes headlines around the world, what is happening in the occupied West Bank? Expropriation of Palestinian land, settler attacks and killings have long been the story of the West Bank, but human rights campaigners and experts say that amid the fog of regional war, attacks are on the rise and Israeli settlers are emboldened. We take a closer look in this special edition of Middle East Matters.

events.title: 'Disaster: Attacks on gas infrastructure, in Iran and Qatar, may set us back several years'
events.summary: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Dr. Adi Imsirovic, Author, Director at Surrey Clean Energy and Lecturer on Energy Systems at Oxford University. According to Dr.Imsirovic, we are witnessing a structural rupture in the norms that have quietly governed conflict in energy-producing regions for decades. Critical energy infrastructure was largely kept off the battlefield, until now. This boundary has now been breached and the consequences will affect the global supply chains for years to come.

events.title: Six nations say ready to boost 'efforts to ensure safe passage' in Hormuz
events.summary: Six Western allies, including Britain, France, Germany and Japan, said in a joint statement Thursday they were ready "to contribute to appropriate efforts to ensure safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz". The statement came as around 20,000 seafarers remained stuck on approximately 3,200 vessels west of the Strait of Hormuz, according to the International Maritime Organization, due to an effective Iranian blockade of the crucial maritime chokepoint. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.

events.title: 'Not our war': Palestinians mourn first dead after Iran missile fire
events.summary: The blast struck without warning in the Palestinian town of Beit Awa, sending a hail of missile fragments through a beauty salon and killing four women inside. In the Israeli-occupied West Bank, the first Palestinian deaths in the Middle East war have sharpened a sense of helplessness as they find themselves ensnared in the crossfire of yet another conflict.

events.title: Paris mayoral race: Socialist frontrunner faces fierce attacks from rivals
events.summary: The Paris mayoral race narrowed on Monday as a former minister hoping to wrest control of the French capital from the left sealed a deal to join forces with a fellow right-winger for the second-round run-off. Her main rival is Socialist former deputy mayor Emmanuel Gregoire, who came out well ahead of her in the first round of voting on Sunday. FRANCE 24's French Politics Editor Marc Perelman tells us more.

events.title: French mayors: Throwing in the sash?
events.summary: The second round of French municipal elections are taking place this coming weekend, but the vast majority of mayors – particularly in small towns – have already been elected. In this edition of Entre Nous, we take a look at what the job entails, why so many mayors say they are exhausted, why so many of them have quit in the past term... and how to properly wear a mayoral sash.

events.title: Middle East war escalates after attack on key Iranian gas field
events.summary: #Israel hit Iranian's part of the South Pars #gas field, the world's largest offshore reservoir #Tehran shares with #Doha, on March 18. In retaliation for the strikes, #Iran ramped up its attacks on Gulf countries' energy infrastructures, sending #oil prices soaring. Israel also reported strikes on its refineries on March 19.

events.title: Last 24 hours show a prolonged Iran war could do calamitous damage to global economy
events.summary: The last 24 hours of eye-wateringly expensive missile duelling over the Persian Gulf has made one point above all.

events.title: China not planning to invade Taiwan in 2027, US says
events.summary: China is not currently planning to invade Taiwan next year, according to US spy agencies.

events.title: Social media blamed for stark decline in young people's happiness
events.summary: Heavy social media use has led to a stark drop in happiness among young people, according to a major academic study.

events.title: Apple issues spyware alert to iPhone users
events.summary: Apple is urging users to update their iPhones after the discovery of new spyware that can take over phones running older versions of the iOS operating system.

events.title: Iranians out in strength as thousands attend funeral for security chief and navy crew
events.summary: As Iran's government buried its fallen heroes, its supporters came out in strength.

events.title: Israel suspected of using war weapon illegally in south Lebanon

events.title: Senegal vows to appeal 'unfair, unacceptable' ruling stripping it of African football trophy
events.summary: Senegal says it will appeal the "unfair, unprecedented, and unacceptable decision" to strip it of its Africa Cup of Nations title after a chaotic final against host Morocco.

events.title: Senegal stripped of Africa Cup of Nations
events.summary: Senegal has been stripped of the Africa Cup of Nations for misconduct in the tournament final, the Confederation of African Football (CAF) has ruled.

events.title: Five sporting results which were overturned - after Senegal drama
events.summary: The Senegal national football side has just been stripped of the Africa Cup of Nations.

events.title: What we know about Joe Kent - the top US counterterrorism official who has resigned over Trump's Iran war
events.summary: Donald Trump's most senior counterterrorism official has resigned, saying he "cannot in good conscience" support the Iran war and claiming the country posed "no imminent threat" to the United States.

events.title: At least 17 dead in fighting on Sudan's border with Chad, aid group says
events.summary: Attacks on a town along Sudan’s border with Chad have killed at least 17 people and injured 123, Doctors Without Borders, or MSF, said

events.title: Pakistan announces 'temporary pause' in strikes against Afghanistan at request of several countries
events.summary: Pakistan has announced a pause in strikes against Afghanistan, saying the decision was made ahead of the Muslim holiday of Eid al-Fitr at the request of Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Turkey

events.title: Mexico arrests suspect wanted in the 2023 killing of Ecuadorian candidate and sends him to Colombia
events.summary: Mexican authorities have arrested an Ecuadorian fugitive accused of helping organize the 2023 assassination of presidential candidate Fernando Villavicencio

events.summary: In Lebanon, renewed hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah and displacement are draining the joy from Ramadan and the run-up to the Islamic holiday of Eid al-Fitr for many

events.title: Iran war expected to prevent Bank of England from cutting interest rates
events.summary: The Bank of England is holding its main interest rate at 3.75% in the wake of the oil and gas price hikes following the start of the Iran war

events.title: Belarus leader hosts US envoy for talks, latest effort to improve ties
events.summary: Authoritarian President Alexander Lukashenko on Thursday hosted a U.S. envoy for talks in the Belarus capital of Minsk, the latest step in the isolated leader’s effort to improve ties with the West

events.title: A real possum appears among plush toy animals in Australian airport gift shop
events.summary: On shelves crammed with cuddly toy native animals in an Australian airport gift shop, one fluffy possum stood out because its brown eyes were moving

events.title: Japan records trade surplus as export growth balances out weak China demand
events.summary: Japan has recorded a trade surplus of 57.3 billion yen, or $360 million, in February, according to government data, reversing from a deficit a month earlier

events.title: Greece launches an animal airlift to evacuate pets and their owners from the Mideast
events.summary: Dozens of dogs and cats have arrived in Athens with their owners aboard a special evacuation flight for Greeks with pets who were trapped by the current war in the Middle East

events.title: Congo and Rwanda agree on steps to de-escalate tensions in eastern Congo
events.summary: Congolese and Rwandan officials have agreed on coordinated steps to de-escalate tensions in eastern Congo where Congo is fighting rebels backed by the neighboring Rwanda

events.title: Five EU leaders seek urgent solution for drifting Russian tanker to avoid environmental catastrophe
events.summary: Five European nations have banded together on Wednesday, seeking an urgent solution for an unmanned Russian tanker currently drifting in the Mediterranean they say poses risk of an environmental catastrophe, the office of Spanish Prime Minister Pedro S...

events.title: A baseball title unleashes the happiness Venezuelans kept bottled up for years
events.summary: Venezuelans pour into the streets in a rare, loud release of joy after their team wins the World Baseball Classic

events.title: Pakistani Taliban announces 3-day ceasefire ahead of Eid truce

events.title: Audiences brace for arguments as sequel to divisive Bollywood spy thriller hits theaters
events.summary: A divisive Bollywood thriller about an Indian spy working undercover in Pakistan that has captivated audiences in the rival South Asian nations for months now has a sequel.

events.title: Ohio firm must pay $22.5 million to mom whose baby died after she was denied work-from-home
events.summary: An Ohio-based company that initially balked at granting a mom's request to work from home during a high-risk pregnancy has been found liable for the newborn's death and ordered on Wednesday to pay $22.5 million in damages

events.title: Officials across the U.S. push to remove Cesar Chavez's name from streets, parks and holidays
events.summary: Allegations that Cesar Chavez sexually abused girls and women, including Dolores Huerta, has forced a reckoning about what to do with places and holidays bearing his name.

events.title: MAGA and progressives unite to push back on AI, drawing scrutiny from Musk and others
events.summary: The organization Humans First, which hosts small group meetings, has gotten attention from Elon Musk and White House AI Czar David Sacks.

events.title: In a Cuban hospital, patients and doctors are hard hit by outages and fuel shortages
events.summary: Cuba’s government granted NBC News rare access inside the Institute of Hematology and Immunology, a research facility in the capital where they treat difficult cases.

events.title: Japanese leader faces a difficult meeting with Trump as he presses for help with Iran
events.summary: Few world leaders seem to get along with President Donald Trump as well as Sanae Takaichi.

events.title: Father of service member killed in Iran war said he never told Pete Hegseth to 'finish' the job
events.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and President Donald Trump have said the families of service members who died in the Iran war told them to finish the job.

events.title: Iran's retaliation is forcing Gulf nations into a stark choice: Stay out or join the fight
events.summary: As Gulf state leaders gathered in a Riyadh hotel this week to discuss the growing Middle East war, strikes from Tehran blasted outside.

events.title: Iran war escalation sets key gas sites, and the world's energy prices, ablaze
events.summary: Iran launched attacks against Qatar's Ras Laffan Industrial City and other Gulf energy infrastructure after Israel bombed the South Pars gas field.

events.title: Trump makes Pearl Harbor joke during meeting with Japanese prime minister
events.summary: Trump invoked Japan's December 1941 attack on Pearl Harbor when speaking about recent U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran during a meeting with Japan's prime minister.

events.title: Missing American student found dead in Spain
events.summary: Devastating news in the search for 20-year-old Jimmy Gracey, who went missing early Tuesday morning after a night out with friends in Barcelona. Family and friends have been desperately searching for clues but today, his body was found by the popular beach where he was last seen. NBC News’ Molly Hunter is in Barcelona.

events.title: Body of missing University of Alabama student found on beach in Spain
events.summary: The body of missing University of Alabama student James Paul Gracey was found on a beach in Spain. NBC News’ Molly Hunter reports on how the family has responded and what officials are saying.

events.title: Iranians attend a mass funeral for officials killed in the war
events.summary: As the conflict with Iran continues, Iranians gathered for a mass funeral to pay tribute to Iran's top security official, Ali Larijani, and other officials that have been killed. Sky News' Dominic Waghorn reports on the Iranian reactions from the funeral.

events.title: Man climbs into viral hippo Moo Deng's enclosure
events.summary: A man has been arrested after he was caught climbing into an enclosure of Moo Deng, the viral pygmy hippo, zoo officials said. The Thai man allegedly entered the enclosure while a keeper was away, and no other visitors were around. NBC News' Sam Brock reports on the situation.

events.title: Israel killed another Iranian leader, but experts say that doesn't mean it's winning the war
events.summary: Israel has again shown its unrivaled ability to assassinate high-level enemies, announcing the killing Tuesday of the man widely seen as running Iran

events.title: Mobile internet blackouts sweep Moscow, leaving residents feeling 'powerless'
events.summary: Moscow has been hit by a major mobile internet blackout as the Russian government tightens restrictions it has touted as necessary to ensure the “security” of its citizens.

events.title: Ukraine's anti-drone tech is in high demand as Iran attacks its neighbors
events.summary: KYIV, Ukraine — As the conflict in the Middle East escalates, Ukraine could prove to be an invaluable trove of battle-tested expertise from its own bitter and costly fight against Russia

events.title: He was a perfect hire — until a U.S. company exposed him as a North Korean plant
events.summary: North Korea has engaged in a wide-ranging effort to place remote workers at U.S. companies in order to funnel money back to its coffers and, in some cases, steal sensitive information.

events.title: Audiences brace for arguments as sequel to divisive Bollywood spy thriller hits theaters
events.summary: A divisive Bollywood thriller about an Indian spy working undercover in Pakistan that has captivated audiences in the rival South Asian nations for months now has a sequel.

events.title: FBI seizes website tied to Iranian cyberattack on U.S. company, hacker group says
events.summary: The Iran-linked hacking group had been quiet since causing significant disruptions to American medical company Stryker.

events.title: Beirut photographer puts himself in the firing line to capture Israeli strikes
events.summary: A missile strike by Israel on a building in Lebanon's capital Beirut was captured in a stunning series of pictures by photographer Fadel Itani.

events.title: Ahead of the FIFA World Cup, pickup soccer games in Mexico's streets are all the rage
events.summary: A group of Mexicans founded "Reta Mexa" on social media, inviting soccer fans to play impromptu matches on different streets in Mexico City.

events.title: Why the Red Sea could be the next choke point for the global economy
events.summary: Iran's Houthi proxy forces in Yemen say they are poised to attack the Red Sea shipping route, risking another peril for the world economy.

events.title: Benjamin Netanyahu tries to dispel viral rumors, fueled by AI paranoia, of his death
events.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has become the subject of a widely debunked but ongoing claim that he is dead, a particularly high-profile example of the clouded and exceedingly fraught information environment created by the mix of AI and social media

events.title: How Lebanon's only international airport stays open amid Israeli strikes
events.summary: Amid regular Israeli strikes, officials in Lebanon are working around the clock to keep the country's only international airport open.

events.title: Trump delays trip to China for 'five or six weeks' while U.S. focuses on Iran
events.summary: President Donald Trump told reporters Tuesday that he would delay his trip to China for “five or six weeks,” officially pushing the major summit after administration officials opened the door to the trip’s postponement as they focus on the war with Iran.

events.title: U.S. embassies ordered to 'immediately' review security as attacks spread
events.summary: The U.S. Embassy in Baghdad was attacked Tuesday, with Iranian strikes previously targeting American embassies in Kuwait and Saudi Arabia.

events.title: Trump says U.S. doesn't need help defending Strait of Hormuz after allies refuse
events.summary: President Donald Trump dropped his push for U.S. allies to protect the Strait of Hormuz from Iranian threats — an about-face that came just one day after he called upon nations to “get involved” so oil tankers can safely navigate the crucial shipping lane.

events.title: Ali Larijani, Iran's security chief and powerful regime insider, is killed in strikes
events.summary: Ali Larijani, Iran’s top national security official and one of the regime's most powerful figures, was killed in overnight strikes.
